reviews.review_text:
Stayed in the new studio cabins for 5 days/ 4 nights. They were clean, modern and had everything you would need for a short stay. Few minor issues with the cabin (no towels on arrival, which was quickly resolved) and the lack of a blind over the kitchen window.

The staff were welcoming and helpful throughout the stay and are available at the reception during the day and have a out of hours service if urgent.

The location was great. Located is a small town on the coast with breathtaking views. I found Yallingup central and it was a 30 minute drive to Busselton and Margaret River. There is a lovely general store/ cafe/ bakery walking distance which were great. It's a nice quiet, respectful atmosphere on the site.

The site is also dog friendly and even allows dogs in the cabins, which was out main reason for choosing the location.

I would highly recommend and we are already planning a return trip later in the year with friends.
Short caravan stay. With 2 small dogs

Well organised check in with plenty of communication via text message . Older, small amenities but clean & maintained during our stay. Had a powered site on the astro turf , nice level surface but the site itself was very small and super tight squeezing the van into position due to adjacent cabins & very narrow road (19.6ft offroad caravan) . The people on the site behind us may aswell of shared our caravans awning as they were so close due to the small sized site! We did enjoy our stay though!

Facilities are below average. The kitchen, as you can see from the picture, is rusty and doesn’t feel safe to use, especially if you have kids. The toilets also have a bad smell from the outside, and considering the number of people staying there, the facilities are insufficient.

Despite it being low season, the place felt overcrowded, with very little space between cabins and camping sites. The location is good, but it does not justify the 53 AUD charged for a powered site, especially since they do not offer unpowered options.
Gute Anlage mit einer genialen Aussicht.

Stayed in one of their new cabins. It was extremely well fitted out cabin. Walking distance to the really spectacular coast line. Great walks near by and the area is still quiet and beautifully underdeveloped. Absolutely magic place.

Huge thanks to Luci for the incredible service! She went out of her way to get us a cabin with an exceptional view that made our entire stay. Her thoughtfulness really stood out,we couldn't have asked for a better experience

Location is great if you want to be near the beach. Everything else is very sub standard for a Tasman park considering the price. The camp “kitchen” is like a tin shed with 1 tiny table and seats for 2 people. I would hate to cook in there, it’s dirty and the shelf under the bench is full of rust with a big rusted hole, doubt it would pass a health inspection. Had 4 nights there and the bathroom wasn’t cleaned until day 3. The sites are small and best suited for tents. The roadway is only 1 way and not wide enough for cars to pass, so if you are hooking off/on any cars have to wait until you’re finished. Ironic that any response from the park is from only good reviews!

Location is stunning but that's where it ends.
Caravan park is that old that it doesn't have room for big vans, the sites are so small. Amenities were updated in 1960 and are dirty.

Cramped, smelly and cold showers, Otherwise, the location itself is spectacular.

After having great experiences at other Tasman camps and paying $130+ Per night for a powered site, I was expecting to be able to have a nightly hot shower and not have to to eat dinner with the humm of sewage lingering in the air each night.

Hot water was hit and miss only managed to get one hot shower during the three night stay.

Site and location brilliant although facilities or pricing need work.
terrible customer service, the lady was very rude over the phone. they’re asking $175 for ONE night for a grass site… almost triple the price of any other caravan park. crazy!!!

I absolutely loved this park mainly because of its location. Grassy sites maybe 100 metres to an amazing beach.
Cafe and surf shop close by. Lovely cliff top walk, close to the pub and general store.

The parks facilities were tidy and well cared for.

Very BASIC site with dirty facilities. Regardless what time I went to the toilet/shower blocks they were dirty. Honestly don't think they were cleaned in the 7 days I was there. Stains on toilets still there when I left, floors filthy. This site is expensive and has nothing to show for it other than the location. It is simply taking your money because it can.
Disappointed

The location in Yallingup is lovely. The beach it is close to is beautiful.

The park itself is average and over $100 per night for 2 people for a powered site. The customer service on arrival was nil. Didn’t even try to welcome us to their amazing location. They were more interested in telling us all the things we weren’t allowed to do, didn’t try and accommodate us and didn’t offer any assistance getting onto our site (bring your patience and your A game). I booked online and then called to check on a site request, my husband celebrating a birthday, was recovering from surgery and was unable to walk to the beach so we asked for a site with a glimpse of the ocean. Nope. Asked again on arrival as these site were empty. Still we were told they were booked. 3 were empty the following day.

It does not take much to help create a fun and happy park. Just a small amount of effort. The pizza couple of Friday worked in the wind and rain and still managed smiles and happy chats. They should be in the caravan park business.

Anyway, we still enjoyed ourselves and the short break away from the confines of the hospital bed.

The ladies shower block was absolutely filthy for the whole time we stayed.  Never cleaned. when I spoke to the manger a young lad, his attitude was “Sorry for that” and seemed inconvenienced.  The filth was not attended to during that day. We were going to book for Jan but at $201 per night for a caravan site the amenties most certainly do ot reflect the price.  Very disappointed.   The  location is good but head office need to see how their  management is oversighting their staff, who are more than probably backpacking and dont give a dam

Tasman Holiday Parks Yallingup Beach is such a hidden gem 🤍
The location is absolutely stunning right by the beach, with beautiful ocean views and that fresh coastal breeze all day long.

Waking up to the sound of the waves here is just next level 🌊✨ Super peaceful and perfect if you’re looking to relax and slow down during your trip.

The park is clean, well maintained, and the facilities are great. Plenty of space for campervans, and everything feels organized and comfortable.

One of the best parts is how close it is to the beach just a short walk and you’re already at one of the most beautiful spots in Yallingup.

Definitely recommended for anyone doing a road trip around Margaret River / South West area. Would 100% stay here again!

Perfectly located opposite the beach at Yallingup with a communal bbq overlooking the beach. Well maintained park with clean amenities. Coffee shop across the road and within walking distance of caves house. Will definitely be visiting again.

Great location, walk over road and check out surf. Great sunset drinks at BBQ area in caravan park or beach front. Nothing to much trouble for the staff, very friendly and accomodating of requests. Thanks to Sven for organisi g us extra nights even though they were full. Toilets and showers were clean.
In the words of Arnie, well be back.

Die 99$ für einen Stellplatz pro Nacht ist übertrieben. Es war nicht mal einer mit Meersicht. Wir hatten einige Campingplätze in West Australien gehabt die viel schöner waren und dies zum halben Preis.
Das Geld sollte mal in die Sanitäranlagen investiert werden. Diese sind veraltet und viel zuwenig für die Menge Leute die Plätze haben. Auch die Camp Kitchen ist alt und sehr klein.
Very small, poorly equipped and maintained accommodation. If it was not for the location, this Holiday Park would have very little going for it.

We travelled as a family and stayed in a Seaview Cabin.  On arrival at the park we entered our allocated cabin only to find the lounge stained and the floor of the shower dirty.  We weren’t confident that the cabin had been cleaned properly so we alerted reception who allocated another cabin for us.  This cabin was much better in terms of cleanliness but it was way too small for 4 people to stay together comfortably for 3 nights.  We felt so cramped and couldn’t wait to leave to be honest - the location is amazing and thank god for the outdoor picnic tables and bbq’s but we feel for nearly $300/night it just wasn’t worth it.
Average

Location, location, location — this park is in a superb spot. A beautiful grassy BBQ area overlooks the beach and is the perfect place to enjoy the sunset.

Unfortunately, the cleanliness of the amenities seriously let the experience down. Basic necessities such as hand soap were empty for days, and there was no toilet paper by the afternoon. I raised this with a cleaner, but it remained unattended. There was vomit left in a sink, and the men’s toilets had faeces on the walls, which is completely unacceptable.

For a caravan park charging $110 per night for a site — and packing guests in very tightly — I would expect a far higher standard of cleanliness and maintenance.
There is no after hours number for maintenance.

Superb location but amenities are so-so. Pricing is becoming extreme. Remember to pack your own toilet paper and carry it with you at all times. It's as valuable as a passport.|The mop bucket worked a treat as a door stop. Never used for mopping.|Yallingup Beach is spectacular, but Tasman is letting WA customers down by continually adding expensive cabins and reducing camping or caravanning sites. We can cope with poor ablution blocks, but we can't agree with the five star pricing.
Lovley spot but very crammed in, could do with more space, i had to move the ute quite a few times so orthers could get there caravans either in or out

This park as switched to dynamic pricing and prices doubled on last year unless they change their pricing after 18 years I will not be back…

And yes the new septic tank was extremely smelly they said they were waiting for carbon filters…
Stayed in the new units up near the caves road. Short 10 mn walk to the beach, close to pub etc. fabulous shops available selling some unusual items.

Stayed 3 nights for a family birthday celebration. Really disappointed that none of the 4 bbq’s overlooking beautiful Yallingup beach worked from Friday morning until we left on Sunday. Many other guests also not happy about it, and some also said their cabin had no hot water. Staff didn’t want to resolve the issue, one was rude and dismissive and just told us to use the other 2 bbq’s. 90 sites and only 2 functional BBQ’s. They can send guests a group text about food trucks and theft, but nothing about bbq’s not working. Previous trips with different management, if we had any issues staff were always friendly, helpful and quick to resolve them. General upkeep is lacking since previous visits

Stayed 3 nights for a family birthday celebration. Really disappointed that none of the 4 bbq’s overlooking beautiful Yallingup beach worked from Friday morning until we left on Sunday. Many other guests also not happy about it, and some also said their cabin had no hot water. Staff didn’t want to resolve the issue, one was rude and dismissive and just told us to use the other 2 bbq’s. 90 sites and only 2 functional BBQ’s. They can send guests a group text about food trucks and theft, but nothing about bbq’s not working. Previous trips with different management, if we had any issues staff were  always friendly, helpful and quick to resolve them. General upkeep is lacking since previous visits.

Beautiful park/ grass area, perfect bbq spots looking over the beach, plenty of park benches, great location just a short walk over the road to the beach, customer service was great. The big downfall is the state of the cabins; we paid $250+ per night for a cabin which had mould, smelt weird, stove top was an old camper electric thing, fridge was so small it couldn’t even fit a 1L milk in the door or half the cold food we took with us, floor was old cheap worn vinyl that felt gritty, fly screen came off the hinges every time we used it, window block out blind chain had fallen off and left on the shelf so couldn’t open the blockout blind, no kitchen cupboard to place dry foods, rolling door for bedroom was loud/ clunky/ came off the tiny floor rail every time we opened it. A family member stayed in a cabin which was ‘renovated’ and still had same uncleanliness experience, bandaid found behind the bin, water pressure in shower and sink was terrible, blue blankets provided smelt like they hadn’t been washed and smelt like previous users perfume, no large kitchen knife provided on butter knives.

Great location but the price you pay for the cabins is not worth it!
Australia gets up early and goes to bed early. Birds wake you up at 5 am.
Amazing location ideal for a small family trip

First time at this park. We had a nice 5 nights because we always make the best of it but was disappointed with the site for our van which was just sand . It was windy at times so everything got covered in sand. There is a beautiful fake grass section for all types of campers and it looked idyllic. The sand sites have shade cloth pads and ours was in terrible condition with holes and trip  hazards and need to be replaced . We had our own ground cover to cover it and keep us out of the sand luckily. . The camp kitchen is tiny and needs to be much bigger  and  the road through the park is full of big potholes. The  ablutions were quite nice but very small. The location is lovely . Lots of bbqs . Located near the cabins. Some improvements and upgrades would make this a great park. Would definitely ask for a fake grass spot next time !!!!
A bit cramped
Very small sites close together and difficult to navigate arriving and departing when towing a caravan and vehicles are encroaching on the roads.|Great location close to the beach.

I stayed in one of the cabins at the park for one night at the end of September.
The cabins are fine, they've recently been updated but it looks like a job done on the cheap.
As it was just one night stay we'd assume all the pre check were done, they were not.
The gas and hot water system was not functioning.
For a $330 p/n stay this is less than ideal.
I realised the issue in the evening, the park does not have an after hours number or any maintenance team. I let the reception know before check out and nothing was done.
I emailed the manager to let them know and they agreed to refund half of the stay, this was a good result.
But as of now nearly one month later, they haven't issued the refund.
I've followed up multiple time, first time the refund was going to come in 2 weeks, then they entered the wrong bank details and now waiting to see what the hold up is.
The Parks management seems to be incompetent.
We had a campsite with electricity and our own - great views for whale watching while having your morning coffee. Short walk to amazing general store and bakery.

Such a beautiful location minute walk to the beach
The park itself is clean and tidy.
The cleaners who clean the ammenities are fantastic.
The caretakers and staff are just lovely.
Great size lots plenty of room
Loved our stay here wish we could have stayed longer.
Short walk to the pub through a tree lined path
Overwhelming sewerage stench and tiny camp spaces
Had a lovely quick one night stay, amazing location with quiet and well maintained grounds, there was no issues with showers or toilets and everything was clean.

This is a rippa of a campsite after day 1 of Cape to Cape hike. Super close to the beach and only a short walk to the Caves Hotel, general store and bakery - all of which are amazing. Lovely service at the front desk. Functional showers and toilets. I think it's geared more for RVs and on site cabins. Would have been nicer to have tent sites away from the vehicles but everything else was terrific.
Cracking location! We had a great time staying here.
We camped here years ago. It's great to see it's still here.
Has everything that is needed, very clean and the staff are very caring and amazing 👏 🤩

Absolutely wonderful impromptu stay as weather prevented us from camping. Sven was a welcome host & our tiny home phenomenal. Highly rated. Modern, close to the beach, well maintained and appointed sites ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Kami menginap pada hari ulang tahunku,cabin mungil yang praktis,indah dengan pemandangan tepat berhadapan dengan pantai .kamar punya semua yang kami butuhkan. Kami akan datang lagi,pertahankan!exelent!

Horrible experience- stayed in cabin 6.
Cabin smelt like and old meat frypan.
The dining room / lounge has food stains everywhere on it.
Mold on all the windows.
And the bed felt like I was rolling off it, the mattress was so shit.
The reception are rude and not people focused.
Major pipe repairs happening behind the chalets no parking access, and still charged full price no discounts applied.

My wife and I visited for the first time over the weekend and had such a wonderful experience. We especially loved our cabin (No. 13). The atmosphere was relaxing, the facilities were well-maintained, and the staff were very welcoming. It was the perfect place to recharge and enjoy nature. We’re definitely looking forward to coming back again! Thanks all.
Beautiful location with gorgeous views, all the facilities that you need and friendly staff.
Home sometimes

We had a great wonderful stay here, and what an amazing view straight outside of our cabin! 🌊

The cabin was small overall for 2 adults and 2 kids, but it had everything we needed for our stay. It was very clean, great shower and comfortable beds. The lawn area outside is fully equipped with benches and bbqs which I can imagine are amazing in summer ☀️

The staff are super friendly and very welcoming, and the campground has all of the amenities you need for a comfortable stay.

I have stayed here a couple of times in the past. Last time was camping!  Never again, we were on a dusty excuse for a site off the maiin track. The chalets have been upgraded and are lovely, 5 star... But for the mere camper the facilities are dismal, the bathrooms and camp kitchen have seen better days. The facilities away from the beach were grotty and one of the loo stalls was constantly wet on the floor. Plumbing is ancient. If they upgraded their ablutions it could be amazing. I think the location has been used as an excuse not to look after the place.

We were off again, this time to Tasman Holiday Parks - Yallingup Beach!

📍 Location & Check-in
🚗 Distance: 258km South of Perth CBD
📚 Check-in: Very simple process, nothing to sign as all paid before arriving. Map and information given on a piece of paper
🕐 In: 2pm for sites & cabins
⏰️ Out: 10am for both sites & cabins

🏕 Amenities Check
🛁 Toilets & Showers: 2 blocks to choose from, one block a little more updated then the other. But both clean and good shower pressure and temperature
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family Room: No family room, but both blocks had a bench top bath in the ladies room
🍳 Camp Kitchen: Is very very basic but everything to get by. BBQ's on a big grass patch over looking the ocean (stunning view & sunsets)
🪁Kids Fun Factor: Nothing for the kids, except the grass area to run and play. But right across the road is a massive play ground and the beach.

🍩 Coffee & Nearby Cafés
☕️ Onsite Coffee Van: Nope, caravan coffee
🧁 Closest Cafe/Food Spot: The office has a small range of drinks, ice cream, ice and gas bottles. Really nice cafe across the road and a surf/clothes shop
🏭 Closest Township: Dunsborough is 10 minute drive away: Coles, woolies, tavern, bakeries everything you need

🏠 Accommodation & Cost
⛺️ Cabins, powered grass sites available
💲We paid $170 for 3 night on a powered site
for: 2 Adults + 1 Child + 1 Infant & 2 Dogs

🐾 Pet-Friendly
🐶 Dogs Allowed: At all times on all sites and kept on leash at all times.

🗣 Heads Up!
🚦 Sites: Very hard to get into, alot of people struggled, tight and narrow road with not much room to manoeuvre
🔥 Fires: Permitted in season (have to have own fire pit and take ash with you)
🌊 Surfing: bring your surf board as so many people surfing daily
🏖 Beaches: so many beautiful beaches and spots to watch the sunsets
🏃‍♂️Running: Mitch enjoyed running on the trails

🚐 Would We Bring Reggie Back?
Absolutely / Yes / Maybe / No
✅ ABSOLUTELY! I'm already planning another visit with a longer stay. It is a stunning place, beaches, sunsets, so many breweries & and so much to see and do....we just didn't have the time.

We are reviewing every place we stay so if you would like to follow our journey give our instagram page a follow @aadriannaconnor

Poor customer service. Tash was so rude to us, we tried to cancel booking, unfortunately we tried to cancel  with the T&C’s 72hrs so then we tried to change booking into another family member. Wasn’t able to do that either. Very unhelpful, abrupt and rude. Not a good representation for Big 4 parks.
Friendly and excellent customer service, thanks Tash. Beautiful spot and loved the Injidup natural spa was spectacular 👌

We loved our stay in Cabin 18.
Space only for parking one car, and we kept the second car near the Reception. Close to the picnic area with fantastic views of the ocean to the west of us. The beds were comfortable and ok for three persons, and perhaps, up to 6 would be a crowd. There were coming facilities.
The ablution block was close by. The facilities were very clean!
Good choice for starting in Yallingup

Nice sites and bbq area with a view over the beach.

Bathrooms were so muddy on the floor and wet on the basins we didn’t shower and found it quite unpleasant. We paid $100 for a night for a powered site over a long weekend and were disappointed with the facilities. No mop in sight to clean up the area myself.

Our most expensive stay in 5 weeks and 2nd worst bathroom facilities unfortunately.

I found  this a hard park to review. We were in a caravan on a powered site, so I can’t comment on chalets. ||The tariff very reasonable for the location , which is awesome, staff were friendly and helpful, so why only 3 stars I hear you ask!  It would have been less on arrival! After a few days I grew to really like to the place, it has a great holiday vibe. Despite my review I would come back! ||We were in the lower section, which has access to stunning views, Sites are a bit tricky to get into as the road is so narrow, but not impossible. They are OK size, but not in great shape. Ours was so uneven it was hard to place chairs without feeling like you were going to fall over. Again not impossible but annoying. The drains were blocked at one stage, but staff came along and sorted it as soon as they were asked. ||The biggest downfall was the shower block, a benchmark for caravan parks, this one is a real let down. It’s old, shower cubicles are far too small, your clothes and shoes always end up soaking wet. But by far  the biggest problem is it’s not cleaned either often enough or thoroughly enough. ||The shower walls were not wiped down in the 8 days we were there, corners never cleaned, it was obvious that they clean with the doors open and never look behind them. Broken window panes, months of cobwebs, peeling paint and damaged roof. The floors were just wiped over with a (well used)  mop, they need to be cleaned properly. I know it’s hard to get staff, but they need to be shown how to clean properly.  It’s probably the most important job in a park, one they should be proud to do well. ||With the right improvements this could be one of the best parks around, it needs some serious TLC.

The only thing that would have made it better is have your own bathroom, but the location is hands down the nicest place. The beach was 100 mtrs from our room, and the best sunset we will definitely recommend this place to our friends and family

Top notch location.  Wander across the road and down the wooden steps straight to the beach. BBQs on the grassed area out front with tables to watch the surf. Couldn't ask for a better place to stay. Lovely staff.

The beautiful location and the inviting BBQ seating area are definite highlights – without them, I would have rated the value for money with just one star. The staff was consistently friendly and welcoming.

The sites are rather small and offer almost no privacy from neighboring guests, which might be acceptable for a one-night stay but not ideal for a longer visit.

I had specifically asked for a well-equipped kitchen, but unfortunately, the available setup was minimal and far below expectations.

The bathroom facilities were clean but clearly outdated and in need of renovation. At this price point, I expected a higher standard and hope the owners will consider investing in improvements for the future.

You simply can't beat the location. Stunning & so chilled. The basic cabins are ok but I would get a beach front if I was to stay longer (just for the view).

Service & value was ok. They had a weekend food truck arrive which did really good burgers.

Your more there for the location. The park did have a good beach front BBQ & picnic table area.

Nice park in good position near beach. Large van sites.
Stayed for 3 nights, we were travelling up from Denmark and were 1 day ahead so I called Tasmania Yallingup office number trying to get a day extra. No answer, called again left message with my details , no return call, called again 3 hrs later left message no return call, called 2 more times. It was the office number , just not good service. Shake up needed .
Love the cabins here, such a great location

We have been coming here for over forty years, so disappointed with the girl at check-in, told us to come back at 1pm. We parked up in the visitor car park, at 12.45pm two caravans came in and were let into the park. I went over and heard them saying to the people, "just as well you booked in now, we were going to close the office for lunch at 1pm"...go figure? So if we were not waiting in the carpark and had come back at Ipm the office would have been closed. Not happy! Office staff need training in customer service!

We book a year in advance for 12 days and request certain sites as we like shade and the sea views between the cabins and pay in full at time of booking.

On the last two occasions we have been allocated sites at the back of the CP which we did not accept!  They then tried to give us a site with no shade at all and the worst site ever. Obviously loyalty counts for nothing.

There is a lot of maintenance to be done in the CP, shade cloth in sites ripped with holes in, the drains in the basins in the ablutions need cleaning, black grime, very unhygienic. Would not go in the shower without thongs on either. The outside of the ablutions not swept. Wash lines need replacing, rusted and lines broken. Site not swept, lolly papers all over the place.

Very sad to see how much it has gone downhill because the CP is in the best location.

We have just come from spending 4 days at Mandalay Caravan Park, Busselton, wow that was absolutely brilliant. Tasman CP Yallingup Management you need to go and take a look at how that is maintained and run and lift your game!

Not sure we will be coming back which saddens us!

The sites are inconsistent. Last week we were on site 45, large, grassed, shady. This week we are on site 70. It’s all dirt, with a tree stump on it, it’s tiny and the smell of sewage from the shack in front is overwhelming. This for $750 a week is pretty poor.
Since Tasman took over they have crammed more sites and haven’t improved the facilities.

Outstanding location. We have been coming here for years, and love the fact that you can wake up and walk straight over the road and get into amazing surf!|Unfortunately the park really needs some maintenance. The roller door seperating our bedroom from the main part of our cabin wouldn’t close properly, and consistently came off its rollers. When I told the staff about it at checkout, she didn’t overly care about it being an issue.|The communal toilets have been somewhat done up but are desperately in need of a deep clean and sanitise. The guys toilets reek!|Overall, it’s a great location, but just isn’t worth the quite high nightly costs at the moment unfortunately.

Had our powered site booked for nearly a year and was staying for 10 nights so a significant amount of time. We were given a terrible site! We only have a 17 ft caravan and my husband who is very skilled had the most difficult time gettting into our spot. As the week panned on all people coming in with caravans near us had the same problem the park is a very tight fit. Our site was right next to the gents whereby we constantly smelt the stench of the gents toilets and the sewerage waste dump point we found out was also on our site when a person knocked on our door to tell us the site would smell for 20 minutes whilst he cleared the sewerage waste into the dumping truck! The park puts tent and swag bookings right next to caravans no separation. So we had 18 year olds in swags all around us carrying on all day and night and we were unable to have any peace. No one patrols the site to tell people to keep noise down or else wise and it completely ruined our family trip having listen to what seemed like 18 year old parties and skateboards around the clock. Such a shame as the caravan park is in a beautiful location. However, based on this experience we will not back.
Perfect location clean facilities and helpful staff.

Usually our favourite spot, but the bathrooms are filthy, to the point it’s unacceptable to use. Floors are wet, soggy toilet paper lying around, bins overflowing. Toilets need urgent attention. Even after they are “cleaned” they are still dirty.

Feeling a little ripped off. Last night up the hill at Tasmans caves camp. Lovely ensuite site $65. Tonight here at the beach with a sandy site with broken patchy slab for $90. Were told it was the last site but there are 2 empty ones near us. Facilities clean but not quite as nice as the other camp. I think this one was only worth $45 - $50 .

Camping setup area was on point
Nice clean and tidy amenities

Will definitely be visiting again
Good location but old and overpriced

Beautiful Yallingup Beach Campsite has been our go-to for years. Sadly this will probably be our last. We have watched the prices rise with the times over the last decade but we were shocked by the prices since Tasman took it over. Very disappointed to see the Seaview cabins have been refurbished to such a poor spec….hardly the reason for such an extravagant price hike. Our cabin looked nice on arrival but within minutes we were shocked at the poor quality and lack of planning and thought and lack of basic amenities that usually come with such expensive accommodation. For the prices they charge you would expect a spa and room service but you only get a transportable.
Great location near the beach but cabin is not clean. There are huge spiders and cobwebs inside the cabin. Other amenities like common toilet and bath are good.
Great place, good price for 4 adults whilst we did the Cape to Cape.

We booked a powered spot for our campervan for one night. Toilets and shower facilities were a bit old but functional and clean. The camp kitchen is tiny and only really suitable for 1 person cooking at one time.
There were lots of BBQs overlooking the sea and the beach is a short walk away.
The sunset viewed from the rocks near the little statue was spectacular.
Overall our stay was fine but nothing spectacular.

Great location, whoever profits from these caravan parks should be arrested. Filthy cabins, no bathrooms in half of them, doors are all off rollers don’t shut prop,very tiny, spiders and cobwebs everywhere, half the community toilets don’t flush ,showers you need thongs on feet also jammed packed with guests.
Because of location  it’s sooo expensive  , would like
see the guest ratio per toilet, I’m sure they would be breaching regulations.  Never here again, pity because lovely location,  unless camping don’t stay here.  It’s really awful.
Lovely place to stay overnight in the caravan park near the beach 🏖

Terrible experience at this caravan park, with unprofessional staff & rude Manager. Tasman Parks “promise” a relaxing experience but this is far from true. Booked for 8 nights, stayed one……! Accused of staff abuse when making a valid complaint.
Not the sort of camping I would normally choose, I prefer campsites in nature but....very good, clean campsite in a perfect position near the beach and restaurant.

In a nice location. We were tenting and the weather was bad so we booked here for a roof over our head to cook and clean. We were pretty let down, especially for what we paid. The kitchen is a tiny little cabin with no seating cover. The small table in the corner was covered in cold fat, you can only fit two people in there at a time so cooking was a bit awkward with other camp users wanting to come in. The bathrooms were unkept and the shower water was cold. They knew we were tenting so wish they let us know the facilities aren’t any good in the rain and we would’ve gone elsewhere. Beautiful views and a nice location. Be good if you’re fully self contained and don’t need to touch the facilities haha. Wouldn’t really recommend otherwise :)
Nice spot, dog friendly.
Location is everything! Basic accommodation with everything you need!

Awesome little caravan park finally one that is not over run with rules! Highly recommend visiting this place.
Great location, friendly staff.

Camped here with friends (tent), so can’t comment on the state of the cabins…

The staff were absolutely wonderful - welcoming, helpful and friendly.
Amenities were nice and clean too!
Walking distance to the beach… can’t get much better!

Bed bugs in the cabin beds… left our holiday covered in itchy bites from the bed, we felt them during the first night almost straight away.. such a shame cause the location was so nice and the staff were good as well but would not return cause of the bed bugs, just hope I don’t bring them back home…

The cabins are basic but bright, clean and you have everything you need. They were also  warm and quiet. The views from the deck were exceptional.
The park itself is very well kept and the bbq area at the front was spotless. The staff were very nice too.
Cape to Cape track is right on the doorstep so hardly needed the car. Loved it!
Good cabin great view.
Awesome spot!

The view from the ocean view cabins is amazing but the cabin itself was a letdown.
The whole park seems to be run by young people and I think they're not cleaning properly. The floor in our cabin was filthy, I wiped up a spill with a wet wipe and it came away almost black.
The curtains are old, musty and feel stiff to the touch from grime in places.
The bathroom smelled of urine and damp. The sink area leaks into the cabinet below which ruined 2 rolls of toilet paper. There were dead bugs all over the ceiling and the shower dripped continuously. I think someone needs to take responsibility and give everything a decent clean!
The TV system was really complicated to use and we just gave up.
I wouldn't stay again in the cabins at this park, they're not worth the money unfortunately. The views are to die for though and proximity to the beach is excellent.
All goods however kitchen needs to be improved

The photos taken were from one of the many ocean view cabins that the park offers.

I was happy with the price I paid and thought it was good value.

The cabin was basic but very clean.

A nice warm shower and good air conditioning.

The staff were very friendly. I will return 100%!

I really in love with this hotel. The view from
Our cabin was just amazing and we really enjoyed our time there. All u need is available in the cabin so no worries. U can cook your food and enjoy it outside while enjoying the view. The check in process also smooth and fast. I love it and I definitely will back here again soon❤️

Hey we stayed overnight at one of the powered ensuite sites and loved it … fantastic staff everywhere getting stuff done .. beautifully clean ensuite… excellent shower

We will be back !

This gem of a spot has gone down hill since it was taken over by Tasman.
The toilet/shower/kitchen blocks need desperate renovation, water was shut off twice during our stay. More attention needs to be paid to the tent only areas, very dry and dusty.
A favourite spot for decades but is in dire need of a facelift.

1. Took money from our account without authorisation.
2.Absolute ripoff for what you get. $75 power site, $90 in hols
3.Small site couldnt put awning out and right next to bins.
4.Showers had no pressure when they were all being used, down to a dribble.
5.lighting near bbq was very dull at night, couldnt cook bbq without own light.
6. Camp kitchen really was a joke, so tiny and grotty.
For the price you pay you would expect the grounds to be emaculate and facilities top notch, but No.
On good note its in a fantastic spot right near the beach where the atmosphere was lovely and the staff were friendly when they eventually answer their phones and in person.
Sorry but wont be back 😞

The worst smelliest/dirtiest cabin I have ever stayed in. Absolutely no response or offer to assist from the staff when we lodged a complaint and request to move even if it had to be a campervan site. There are some new cabins that looked ok from the outside but ours was very old and smelt like mould and stale ciggerettes the windows were covered in grubby hand marks. The smell was just the worst and to top it off they charged $250 a night we were absolutly gob smacked at the price for what we got. The only saving grace was the view near the BBQs everything else is extremly old.
Great camping, best BBQs I've ever seen! Amenities are very clean.

Beautiful location and very friendly and helpful staff. However, the place is filthy. Showers and toilets were disgusting and rarely cleaned effectively. Camp kitchen was tiny, and the park being so full, seriously needs an upgrade. Another beautiful location really just surviving off its prior reputation and not investing in the facilities. Also, staff member had a dog on site that barked constantly when left unsupervised, super annoying when trying to relax or sleep.
This caravan park is incredibly awesome  - the staff, the amenities, and the location are superb.

Been coming to this campsite for over 10 years now, I met my partner here whilst over looking the ocean at sun set.

We all enjoy coming here for our family getaway and have always felt safe and love the experience of been here, so many good memories for our children 🙌

The only thing that has changed is that it is more dog friendly, we feel they should have a small fenced off area set aside for a playground rather than relying on the park over the other side of the main road.
To bring back the codes/ digilocks on the toilets and showers to stop random people  walking in and non paying public who use these facilities.

Thank you 🙏

We absolutely loved this caravan park, my only regret is not staying longer than 3 nights. This area is absolutely full to the brim with glorious nature.
We all loved the snorkelling, which is gentle and so many fish to spot in the shallows.
As for the actual park, it's very well set-up. It's super clean, staff are friendly and helpful. We also had a leak in our van that the park's handyman helped us with at no charge. We will definitely stay here again.

Just spent a week camping here absolutely perfect!
Read some reviews about bathrooms but we found them spotless every time except when left dirty   by other campers
The young crew cleaning them did a great job
Girls in reception lovely too
Location is spot on right across from beach
And the quietest park we've ever stayed at
Thank you we will be back

Been coming here for years but this site has gone seriously downhill. Bookings were a complete mess and we were given the wrong site, the place seemed overcrowded and overbooked, the bathrooms are disgusting and never clean, 10pm noise curfew was not adhered to, and now they allow dogs on site! Not great for kids running around and who are afraid of dogs. We love Yallingup but have decided after years of coming here, we will be going elsewhere from now on.

If you care about the safety of your children do not book here. Facilities in need of a serious clean, both bathrooms have had the code lock system broken allowing anyone to enter and overpriced at $85 per night on a dirt site. We had items stolen from the bathroom by someone who wasn’t staying at the camp, when reported to staff they couldn’t care less.

I am considering reporting this to consumer affairs as I am beyond appalled and horrified with this.

Powered site was very dirty and unkept . Toilet facilities in need of a thorough clean also laundry . Old camp kitchen was more like an old shed and needed to be demolished .Definitely overpriced for 2 adults in. November $65 . Certainly won’t be back
My favorite escape location

Been coming here for 15 years always a pleasure!
The Marvelli's site 31

I stayed a week in a tent site with my 2 small kids.

Location is amazing, just across one of the best beaches of the area.

Camping is child-friendly but the facilities are horrible: old, dirty and tiny kitchen with only a cooktop and 2 small old and dirty fridges. The cloths provided to wipe the sink have been hanging there since when we arrived and they were already dirty a week ago.

Only 2 ablutions blocks present in the all camping with 3 showers and 3 toilet for each sex.

The cleaning crew is made up of very young girls who doesn’t know anything about cleaning as I saw them with my own eyes how they cleaned the toilets, basins and kitchen… basically just a wipe with a cloth and some detergent spray… that was all!

I honestly think that, whoever owns the place, couldn’t care less of how it is kept or looks like as they are always fully booked due to the amazing location the camping is built on.

However, to justify the prices that they ask, a renovation is needed because customers like me won’t come back next time if the facilities are still as poor as they are.

I agree with other reviews where it said that the only plus is the girls at the reception who are very helpful and kind.

Spectacular location, but this park very poor considering being in prime WA site.

Facilities for caravans poor. No ocean views , small and difficult sites. Older ablution block far too small and tired , cannot lock ladies toilet cubicles as doors dropped past catches. Camp kitchen just a pokey shed.

Staff are very friendly and helpful.
Caves House is only a short walk up the hill.
Beautiful lagoon, beach, surfing.

We had a lovely couple of days.

Great location. The park grounds are maintained to an acceptable standard. Quiet hours are adhered to making it easy to sleep.
The park is let down by the poor standard of cleaning. Bathrooms, laundry need deep cleaning. Years of cracked on scum and dirt.
The kitchen is a health hazard- disgusting - it should be closed.
really enjoyed our stay, incredible location and everyone at reception was friendly and helpful

5 days in paradise, sea view cabin with the most beautiful green lawned place to have our barbie every night with a spectacular vista. Friendly staff, always attentive and ready to go the extra mile. Very clean everywhere, sparkling barbie daily. We have already booked our week for next year. Thank you to the managers and their team for making our 5 day break WONDERFUL.

Facilities for caravans poor. No ocean views , small and difficult sites. Older ablution block far too small and tired , cannot lock ladies toilet cubicles as doors dropped past catches. Lovely area in front of some chalets ,, however dangerous broken path light not attended to or marked as a hazard !
This park very poor considering being in prime WA site. As a plus lady at reception very pleasant and professional

We stayed here for a total of 4 nights and not once was the toilet/shower block cleaned. Dirty marks on the walls and shower, hair on the floor and disgusting floors that remained in the same unhygienic state throughout the duration of my stay, despite discussing and showing management twice (they even took photos) yet it still was not cleaned. On one occasion I saw a cleaning sign out the front for the entire day, yet the condition of toilets/showers remained the same. It’s so disappointing for such a beautiful location.
Amenities are a bit old  but the location is incredible.  Bbqs with view of the sunset over the ocean!!!

Aside from ablution block overflowing (not the park's fault), generally the cleanliness was terrible. Hair and band-aids in the showers, bins overflowing, tried to report it to counter staff and they were disinterested at best. Good location close to the kids park and track to the pub was convenient, but wouldn't stay there if cleanliness and service was a priority.
Beautiful seaview from our ensuite cabin
My own caravan used so rooms and service not applicable
Relaxing,great place
The staff are amazing 👏

We stayed in the Sea side cabin for 2 nights. The stay was wonderful and memorable. Sea side cabins have very nice view of the ocean and also their is a way directly to the beach.
My lil daughter liked the room very much as it had bunk beds.
Overall a very nice hotel in a awesome location.
Friendly creating from lady at reception. Park is quiet and tidy

First time at this park and it's location is just perfect. Entry at night can be a bit tricky as it's not well lit. We stayed in a ocean view cabin and it's location and view to ocean was fantastic. The cabin however was somewhat disappointing, on the plus it was clean with a great a/c heater, comfy bed with loads of extra blankets and pillows and nice little porch to sit out on and enjoy the sunsets. The negatives was that it was very dated, no lounge area, TV mounted high on the kitchen wall, uncomfortable chairs and a tad dated. The worst was that the curtains didn't close properly in any room especially the bedroom with the window backing onto the rest of the park. Was it value for money and would we stay there again - yes and sure would, we'd just go a little more prepared.

I have NEVER in my 32 years come across someone with better customer service than Hesper.
She was extremely friendly, helpful and accommodating on our arrival.
If everyone in customer service was like her, this world would be a better place. 💫

Oh and the caravan park & location is also great, but that's obvious on arrival 😍⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Stayed on a site so no comment on rooms but the cabins looked  great. Excellent customer service, friendly staff ,excellent on site ensuites with separate shower /toilet facilities. Will be choosing a dog friendly cabin(there are 5) next stay. Close to pub, general store and bakery. Good walks.Great central place for tourists. Do yourself a favour and try this place out.
Great park to stay at. Friendly and helpful staff. Well maintained facilities and a really good location/base for exploring the region and attractions.

We had such a great stay at Tasman Holiday Park in Yallingup! Mary on the reception desk was so welcoming and friendly! The cabin we stayed in had everything we needed and was super cosy. The beach is only a 10mins walk from the accom and the water was gorgeous 😍

Lovely campsite. Very clean and big kitchen area.
Special mention to the staff, we had a big group staying next to us making quite a lot of noise on the first night. I called the out of hours line and within 5 minutes someone came out and asked them to quieten down. They also gave the group a courtesy call the next day so we had no further issues during our stay. Thank you guys!
We found your campsite very clean, great location and had a great stay
Great Camp site

Stayed here for a few nights, great campsite with a really good camp kitchen and regularly cleaned toilets/showers.

Mary checked us in and is probably the nicest person we’ve ever met! She’s made for customer service!! Couldn’t have done more for us, she’s a credit to the business😊

We have stayed at the Tasman Caves Road Park now three times and loved it each time.
Wonderful amenities, lot’s of shady trees, plenty of space to enjoy nature and attentive customer focused helpful staff.
We’ll be back!
Being pet friendly is a big plus for us, our beloved Springer loves it too!🐾

Very BASIC site with dirty facilities. Regardless what time I went to the toilet/shower blocks they were dirty. Honestly don't think they were cleaned in the 7 days I was there. Stains on toilets still there when I left, floors filthy. This site is expensive and has nothing to show for it other than the location. It is simply taking your money because it can.

We had been staying here for a few days. We were just booking as we went, the price was around $39 a night for unpowered. On the last night we extended right at the end of the day and it cost us $57 for the unpowered site! Called up the next day to try and understand the cost difference. We were told it was because it was the last site. A bit disappointing to pay so much when we hadn’t paid that much in previous days. Not a fan of the “dynamic pricing”. Ladies in the office were lovely but would have been nice if a refund was offered to keep the price paid the same across all days.
Nice caravan park with all the facilities you need. Looks like they had a seperate area for families so it was quiet. Girls at the desk were super helpful and friendly.

Had a nice stay in a cabin. Cabin was a bit modern for my taste  but very clean and presentable. Staff were very kind, and let me check out a half hour late as I had a zoom interview. Mary and her friend with the blue eyeliner at reception are very friendly and knowledgeable and explained everything about the camp ground. I would definitely recommend this location, but would advise to book well ahead of schedule.

Spent 2 nights here, it’s a nice camp site with good toilets/showers and a large camp kitchen area. However the powered and unpowered sites are small and very close together. Feels like everyone is on top of each other. The first night was rammed, luckily the second night was very quiet with no one around us.
This was the most expensive camp we stayed in as well.
We love coming to Caves Caravan Park, terrific location & excellent facilities, especially with their new pool deck!

Very bad experince , one night stay they charge $423.00 for very small tiny cabin ,it's has 6 beds but you cant't move around as much as so tiny  it is,  it was  way over price,  while other places they offer 3 bedroom cottage or fancy stay in any resorts close to  this price ,  we never back again
this place  seems okay for caravan park or tint only

Stayed here over the Australia Day long weekend. Was a great place to stay for a quick getaway with a family of 4. Location is magic, with a skip across the road to the General store, bottle shop and Caves house pub. I would say it’s a bit pricey compared to AirBnb’s etc. Games room was a bit of a miss for the kids as they’ve had issues with vandalism and kids being flogs. Pool should be open longer, says daylight hours, the sign says 6pm, then the check in says 5pm

We were camping, Great camp kitchen, good bathroom facilities, beautiful pool best spot straight over the road from Caves house and walking distance to Yallingup beach(also only 5 minutes to Dunsborough)
Good spot for a night or 2
Cabin was very confortable with some amenities. Staff very friendly

Overall it is a nice leafy caravan park, staff are super friendly and it was a nice enough stay, however, the ensuite we had was not like the website, it was old and shower needed a good scrub, some of the sites are tight to get into and slabs need some work, the gardeners large dogs were running loose a couple of times.

The park is dog friendly and it is close to a tavern, bakery and coffee shop. But we couldn’t find anywhere we could take the dog for a walk. I understand there are national parks there but even the park was a dog free zone. This made it difficult and not the caravan parks issue.

We booked the wrong caravan park as their website is a little confusing and kinda merges the beach one with the one on the hill, across the toad from the tavern. Paid $70 a night, booking well in advance but found if we had waited to the week of the booking we would have saved $10 a night.

It was okay as a park but probably would stay in Dunsborough next time.

Recently stayed 7 nights in their deluxe cabin with my husband and 10 month old.
Cabins are clean and modern, a bit on the smaller side of cabins we've stayed at before. We asked prior to arrival how we'd d
Go about fitting a porta cot in the second bedroom between the bunk beds and were told it should fit. However when we arrived, the gap between the beds was barely half a metre wide. So thankfully we were able to fit the porta cot in the master even though was still a little squishy. Once our little one graduates to a bed, these will be totally fine but don't recommend for families travelling with a baby. But otherwise we loved our stay. All the kitchen wear worked, shower was lovely and spacious and warm. WiFi worked well, a bit slower in the evenings which is when everyone logs on for the day. Fully equipped cabin however not much pantry space so can't store your groceries for a week. Park is quiet, clean and well maintained. Do get a bit of music from the caves house across the road on weekends but music shuts off around 9pm so really quite respectful. Honestly loved our stay. This is our second stay with them. First time in tents and this time in cabins due to the baby. Despite the minor things here and there, our stay was wonderful and would always return here.

clean cabin,needs TLC that's for sure,floor was not the best in main room,needs new beds and lounge,bathroom had lots of mould and smelt like urine.it was not worth what we paid a night,the campers were close and noisy,they should be in a different area I think.I would not stay in those cabins again.

We had a wonderful time, Mary at reception was the most fantastic helpful person, she went above and beyond for our stay, I put wrong dates for our stay and couldn't  change them, but Mary sorted it all out for us. Our cabin was beautiful  but the drains were blocked, so Mary upgraded us to a Deluxe Cabin. We would recommend staying at the Tasman Caves caravan park to everyone. 10/10 ..

staff amazing
very helpful
holiday Park very clean
well done
went to the caves and they were interesting to say the least

Be careful of all the seemingly stellar reviews. Personally, I think this is a very sub standard park. The sites are absolutely tiny albeit most with ensuites however they are incredibly dated and not well maintained. There are a handful of reasonable sized sites however they seemed to be reserved for regular guests of the park. I was assigned a site the size of a shopping centre car park that I couldn't open my awning on, despite giving accurate information on the size of my set up, staff were rude and unhelpful when trying to resolve this matter.

WOULD NOT RECOMMEND

We stayed at an ensuite site that was just perfect.  Had a good shower with hot water which for some places they did not have much hot water. Location was excellent and close to everything.  Had a general store accross the road a restaurant and a bakery. The beach was a few mins away walk and the people at reception were wonderful,  I was talking to Mary alot she is super nice and super helpful. There is only one dryer as most of the new cabins have a washer and dryer in them, also wifi we could not connect as they do need an upgrade for wifi. But overall a great 4 nights that we stayed. Margaret river is only 35 minutes drive away as well and you have to visit the ngini Caves and Canal Rocks if u you in Yallingup.

We stayed in a cabin here while cycle touring. The cabin was excellent and had everything we needed.

The welcome at reception was fantastic and the kind lady gave us loads of useful information about the area etc.

The park had all the facilities you’d expect but to a very high standard. Further improvements were in progress so it going to get even better.

I’d definitely stay again and would definitely recommend this site. 5 stars all the way!
Such a nice place to stay, we are in a powered site for two weeks while we work in Yallingup and could not be happier.

We were absolutely delighted with our stay at Tasman Holiday Parks – Yallingup Caves! 💖 We were lucky to book one of the brand-new cabins, and it exceeded all expectations — modern, spotless, and beautifully maintained both inside and out.

The cabin had everything we needed for a perfect getaway. The location is also fantastic — just a short stroll to a great local pub, a handy convenience store, and the post office. Best of all, we were only steps away from some incredible bakeries… we had the most delicious sandwich we've ever tasted there! 🤤

This park is ideal for families, couples, or anyone just looking to relax and enjoy the beautiful surroundings. Highly recommended — we’ll definitely be back!

Perfect spot for cape to cape trail trek in the quieter periods. Shaded, peaceful, close to ocean and surrounded by nature. Cabins clean and comfortable, fully equipped. Staff amazing. Highly recommend
Great spot
Great place to stay! Clean amenities, a spacious kitchen, and lovely spots for caravans. Mery was very helpful and welcoming.

Very nice stay down here for us, staff was exceptionally friendly, all questions answered looked after the whole booking very well. Amazing camp site with heaps of wildlife, across the road from the pub, bakery, store. TV, games room and a good based to head out on day trips.
Always clear, clean and flat camping sites. We usually get a powered "bush" site so we're amongst some trees 😁
Really well looked after rooms, staff welcomed us with a smile and nothing was too hard for them during our stay.

Wow .. this place is amazing!
Location fabulous, the room was INCREDIBLE.

Do yourself a favour and book here. We didn’t want to leave. We only stayed one night while we were travelling and both adults and kids agreed we are coming back to stay longer!!

Fantastic.
Great location just across the road from caves hotel. Good, clean facilities, only thing lacking was storage space in kitchen fridge is very limited

What an absolute perfect place for a family getaway. The location was superb, the facilities fabulous and the customer service was next level.
We are already planning on our next family holiday at this wonderful place.
The coffee across the rd at the general store was amazing and the fresh baked goods at the bakery, were sensational.
We would highly recommend staying in this little piece of paradise.
Thanks to all the staff who ensured our stay was as wonderful as it was ❤️
Nice park but lot of prickles in grass area

Wir waren 5 Nächte auf diesem CP. Lage 👍, wir konnten uns unseren Platz aussuchen was toll war. Es ist sehr staubig auf dem Platz aber gut damit konnten wir leben. Aber die Waschräume 🤮, nicht nur das sie nicht richtig gereinigt wurden es war überall schwarzer Schimmel. Und nach dem Duschen war alles feucht weil der Dampf nicht vernünftig abziehen konnte. Schade, ansonsten ein guter Platz für einen guten Preis.
Awesome staff awesome location love it
There are plenty to do many wineries along Caves Rd. Nice caravan park we're campers are separate from the caravaners. Also it is a no smoking or vaping on the premises.

Pricey but very nice ensuite caravan sites with own 70s style roomy bathrooms with separate shower, toilet,sink & very hot water, lots of grass and trees for shade. Camp kitchen has everything you need, Very friendly and helpful staff member at check in.

We recently stayed in one of the non-refurbished cabins for 5 nights and found it to be a rip-off for the price of nearly $300pn.
The cabin was poorly maintained and smelt of dog (especially the couch).
There was black mould on the ceiling above the shower recess, the towel rails fell off the wall when we placed towels on them, the back of the bathroom door looked like it had been chewed by a dog, the drain in the wash basin was practically non-existent, one of the lights on the veranda did not work and the floors in the cabin were filthy.
We could not connect to the wifi because the network was mismanaged with all the client leases (IP addresses) still held by previous customers.
Do yourself a favour and look for somewhere else to stay.

We first came here in September 1982 as relatively newlyweds and loved it. We came back for a 5 day stay 42 years later in Feb 2025 (we still feel like we are newlyweds) and still loved it. A quiet location, lovely and green even in summer and lots of trees. As we came down for a wedding we had a powered site with an ensuite for convenience. The camp kitchen is the "Taj Mahal" of camp kitchens from what we have seen in our caravanning and the other facilities are first class too. Staff were very friendly and helpful. Nothing was too much trouble. Thoroughly recommend a stay here.

Great and private amenities (seperate little bathrooms with shower, toilet and sink inside), lots of nice grass-sites and trees for shade.
Friendly and helpful staff

Location is beautiful

Camp spots are small not much room for a car and a tent at all ,
So if your going in a group it’s okay you car share of your next to each other
Also power on the powered site  can be very far away make sure your extension leads are very long

but the camp site facilities are dirty the bbq are not cleaned enough so after dinner use waking for breakfast they are still dirty from dinner use I didn’t see any cleaners in the camp area seem to only focus on the chalets the camp kitchen needs to be wiped over regularly not just once a day

The share  bathrooms and toilet smell are dirty they need more cleaners and they need to pay attention to cleaning them more regularly and thoroughly wasn’t enjoyable at all

Tasman Holiday Parkes Caves Yallingup stayed 5 nights caravan site. January stay.
Location is great as a base to visit many attractions close by. Walking distance to beach, caves, the general store and a lovely little bakery. Not far from Margaret River town, Busselton and Dunsborough.

Caravan site was a huge let down. Had a lot of difficulty reversing caravan in as lots cars parked on the side. Not much room to move to park caravan in or leaving. Site was broken slabs of concrete and very uneven. Not much room for car to be parked. Had to park on kerbside.
Complained at office however was told to email.
Private ensuite needs some renovation, very old, lots rusted door frames, cob webs, dark and gloomy and mould.
Ensuite was large, that a bonus.

Paid $149 per night for a caravan site!Couldn't find anywhere else at the time and booked this place thinking it would be prime with that price however not so.
Park needs some serious love and care as in a lovely location.
Unfortunately wouldn't return.

Beautiful location across the road from a bakery, general store and lovely hotel and restaurant. Camp sites under trees are beautiful and shaded. Bathrooms dirty, soap scum and mold everywhere. It's clear cleaners only swish around the floor and don't wipe down tiled walls. A little bleach would go a long way.
Zeer nette en mooie camping met eigen sanitair.

Lovely park. Individualised bathrooms - the 70s brick ones are more spacious and easy to use. The newer ensuite public bathrooms are pokey and small. Great camp kitchen. And eating area. Friendly staff. Access to bbqs and beach walking distance.

Seemed like a well equipped park that wasn't too large, however our 2 bedroom chalet was around $300 a night (non-school holiday time).  For that price I would have expected a far better standard of cleaning.  There was food/hair/rubbish from previous (how many?) guests under the couch and under our bed.  It seems that they do a very quick changeover between guests and standards aren't that high.  Compared to caravan park chalets at other WA holiday parks I have stayed in, this seemed smaller, less well appointed and poorer value for money.
The ensuite at the caravan sites was appreciated.  Nice to go to the hotel and local business for meals

Tasman Holiday Park Yallingup
am 25.11.2024 eingecheckt in der Reception
wurde ich von einer sehr freundlich Dame empfangen, ich bekam alle Infos die man braucht.
Der Tasman Park ist sehr gut organisiert, die Stellplätze sauber und groß genug für alles!
mein Fazit... einer der besten Plätze überhaupt in Western Australia

Tout faux
2 car parks identiques, même société même nom. À la réservation vous pensez réserver près de la plage, raté vous êtes sur la colline.
À la réception, vous dites qu’il est stressant de recevoir 5 mails comminatoires ces 3 derniers jours vous demandant de payer et vos coordonnées de CB alors que vous avez payé à la resa. Oh pardon! La CB c’est pour la caution. Pourquoi puisqu’il n’y a pas d’état des lieux à l’entrée ?
Bungalow pour 5, les seuls à avoir une SdB. 4 chaises, 2 fourchettes …? Les ustensiles de cuisine ne sont pas nets, à relaver…
Un occultant qui ne marche pas…
On a plus de chance de se cogner que de se perdre dans cette minuscule cabine de douche.
Les terrasses donnent sur les autres occupants : apéro en public.
Soyons positifs, c’est calme.

It is a place where if you forget something in the room after checked out it will be gone immediately.  No staff can know or find your belongings, even though no one has stepped foot into the cabin since we left. Not impressed with the customer service after all. (Except Mary who's very nice and very helpful)
Easy to check in Mary was very helpful and polite. Camp grounds were great, the camp kitchen is top notch, the showers were a little messy and could do 2 cleans a day. Also dog friendly.
Nice place, great service
Paid and was confirmed foa a powered ensuite concrete slab and got a dirt pad with trees obstructing us putting out the awning.  Toilets and showers disgusting . Wont be back !!

Very friendly staff. Mary was so helpful. Wet and muddy but can't control the weather. Unfortunately only ensuite sites available do to major works happening. Unable to put the awning or a chair. Outside . Very small site for our 21ft van
Ensuite sites dated but very clean.steps up to ensuite squashed us in further.
Good luck with the new cabins.
$53 per night with Big 4 membership. A bit overpriced.

Thankyou for having us.

This is a really lovely park set in picturesque Yallingup, opposite an excellent bakery and pub and within easy drive of wineries etc. The grounds are well kept. Sites are spacious with concrete slabs. Amenities are modern and clean. Staff are exceptional. Would have preferred unpowered site but otherwise could not fault this park
Great cabins and great views of the beach beautiful sunsets and nice bbq

Our favourite campsite on our whole 3 week trip! Immaculate facilities, staff are really helpful and let us pick our site.
Literally 3 min drive from Yallingup Beach with a pub, general store and bakery a 2 min walk away. Couldn’t be a more ideal location.
Thank you!
Great little park with Amazing staff. Had an ensuite site which was handy, old but clean. Great location with a great little coffee place plus Caves House  across the road
I love this hotel. Beautiful view from my room. Have the kitchen utensils as well so u can cook and enjoy your food while watching the beautiful view❤️🥰

Nice caravan park with all amenities, the staff was very friendly and helpful. Our site was nice size with grass. Some of the sites are quite small and difficult to get into but staff do assist were required. Close to bakery and pub, basically across from the park.

A park with all the amenities at your door step. Only a hop skip and jump from the store and pub.

Huge camp kitchen.

Just the place I needed for my 2 night stay to start my Cape to Cape hike.

Bathrooms were well appointed, didn’t have to wait for one very long if at all.

Great location, fabulous ammenities. However……. We paid top dollar for powered site $75, the power was an indoor 4 pack running from an extension lead from a shed which did not support 15 amp power that is  usually required at a caravan park, just glad our van didn’t short circuit ⚡️
One night and I’m outa here!

Booked a camp site here in the very last minute before Christmas.

The bathroom and toilet were clean but can be better lit.

Kitchen facilities - there's one microwave, a couple of kettles and one fridge to share amongst the campers.

Every Friday there's a food truck that comes to sell pizza.
Simply the best!! Mason, Sarah and Mary extremely helpful. They rock!

My family had a great experience camping at a powered tent site.  Loved the location, which was central to the places we wanted to visit and opposite the General Store, which made great coffee.

The kids enjoyed the bounce pad and swing.  Showers and facilities were modern and clean.  Just felt that the kitchen area could do with a more regular clean and some of the bins needed emptying more frequently.

Really enjoyed this holiday park and would recommend.

We stayed here for 4 nights with our 2 boys. I have mixed  feelings about his campsite.

Location is great, the ladies on reception are so helpful And friendly. The park is well maintained.

We stayed in a bush camp, it was very dusty so be prepared for everything to get filthy. For a couple of nights it’s fine just make sure you have a broom/vaccum/blower and enough clothes! Or really I would opt for a grass site.

the bush site we were in was a good size but due to the layout meant we had so many  campers all around us, 3 down one side 2 down the other and 2 backing on to us. So there was just a lot of noise around. Tho I will say whilst we stayed everyone was respectful
Of the noise curfew of 10am.

The unisex toilet/showers. It feels like the kitchen and toilet block is relatively new but they did t have room for a male and female block so instead there is about 6 unisex toilet/showerRooms and 2 separate toilets. Which where always busy and meant you could be showering after someone else just used the toilet or going to the toilet after someone showered and it was humid and hot and wet. I didn’t love this format, but the block
Was new and hot water is a pro!

Usually when we camp my boys love to ride round with other kids, no one was really cycling maybe cos it’s dirt hilly rocky
Roads so they played on the pillow or so we thought - in reality they where sneaking off to the tv room. We go away to escape screen time so personally didn't love tv was so readily available - especially you tube tv!

So in summary - the site is good, clean
And well ran but there were things that didn’t suit our preferences as a family so we wouldn’t be likely to return but rather try another site in the area.

A great spot for camping. Lots of trees, excellent facilities. The one gripe for families is the tv room and game room. No parent who doesn't bring their own tv and entertainment wants their child to disappear to the tv room while at a camp site. a tv in the camp kitchen is ok. A dark tv room is not.
Fantastic location and very friendly staff
Hier war es eigentlich ganz schön, es hatte einfach zuviele Fliegen...Die Sanitären Anlagen waren leider nicht so sauber....schade wenn die Leute ihren eigenen "Dreck"nicht putzen

Had a few days camping here. Really nice site with great facilities. Friendly reception staff who were very helpful. We hadn't booked just dropped in and found a roomy spot. In a great location for wineries and beaches - highlight being Castle Bay beach just up the road from Dunsborough.
Great location to Caves hotel Cafe and beach. Quiet and shady.
Our group stayed for 3 nights. Great location! Customer service was fantastic, facilities are great, clean and convenient. Would definitely come back here again. Loved our stay, thank you
Nice site, well situated with good facilities. Slightly spoiled by music noise from the local pub which went on far too long.
Great experience.Lady at the reception was super awesome !

Stayed here for one night while on a road trip this week.  The reception staff were very friendly and informative about the local area.  The cabin was just perfect for the two of us and backed onto bushland.  Everything you needed was there in the kitchen.  Beds were comfy. Lovely location.

12 days. What a wonderful place to stay in a natural environment. Hand fed ring necks & a possum. But all modern facilities. Excellent camp kitchen where they did bacon and egg rolls for Father's Day (free).  The most amazing staff, all very lovely, helpful & happy. Good vibe from others exploring far and wide. Caves Hotel across the road did a great fish & chips, dog friendly. Extended our stay, sorry to leave. Caravan site was easy access, but they are happy to help if it’s a bit harder. So much to see cannot list it all. Got a car service with Auto Masters in Busso, excellent. Eagle heritage excellent. Simmo  ice cream a must. Otherwise too much  to mention. Ps swings and roundabouts pizza to die for lol. PPS thank you Mary
Great value for $ cabin 👌 but the most awesome part of this stay was the friendly helpful amazing staff management should b proud

Tasman Caravan park is in a beautiful spot and just across the road is a the Caves Hotel, bakery, bottle shop, cafe / food, trails to caves and so much more! Best just to experience it for yourself but you will need to be quick to get a spot. Did I say how good the service there 😍

Great location  older bungalows comfortable. Clean with heating.
Check in staff friendly and helpful..
Would stay again...

Lovely cabins, friendly staff and you can take your dog which is an added bonus. Our Greyhound had a great time. Fabulous location just opposite Caves House and a 2-minute drive to Yallingup Beach. 5 Stars.
off-season, unpowered, rooftoptent = 35$ sehr fair. very good campkitchen, nicely, open, roofed area, good bathrooms (perfect for families)

Lovely and well run Caravan park. Check in was welcoming. Everyone knows quiet time is 10pm. Toilet and bathrooms clean and tidy. Across the road from Caves house and local shop. Coast is shortish walk. We would definitely recommend and stay again.
